issues.title: [Help wanted] Default window decorations color
issues.next_action_reason: Re-anchor this popular help-wanted thread: explain the RAIL server-side titlebar vs GTK_THEME distinction and invite a scoped PR; add the 'help wanted' label to match the title.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks for keeping this alive — it stays one of the most-requested decoration issues. To summarize where it stands for newcomers: WSLg draws server-side (RDP RAIL) decorations for the title bar, so an app's own GTK_THEME does not control the titlebar color; apps that draw client-side decorations (many GTK apps) follow the theme, while apps that rely on the WM/server titlebar (JetBrains, xcalc) get the default light frame. This is tagged help-wanted — a PR that plumbs a dark-titlebar preference (or honors the host's dark-mode / a `.wslgconfig` setting) through the RAIL decoration path would be very welcome. If anyone investigating wants pointers on where the titlebar color is chosen in the compositor, reply here and we can help scope it.

issues.title: Self-referential WM_TRANSIENT_FOR wedges WSLg GUI session for a distro
issues.next_action_reason: Acknowledge the excellent repro and confirm the self-referential transient should be rejected defensively in the compositor/Xwayland path.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks @IMXEren — the xtrace makes this very clear. A client setting `WM_TRANSIENT_FOR` to its own window is malformed, and WSLg/Xwayland should defensively ignore a self-referential transient rather than leaving every subsequent surface `IsUnMapped` for the whole session. We'll trace where the transient cycle stalls mapping in the weston RDP/Xwayland path.
issues.owner_evidence: Xwayland/weston window-management path; route to weston-mirror backend maintainers.
issues.owner_fallback_reason: Xwayland/weston window-management path; route to weston-mirror backend maintainers.

issues.title: Azure Linux dependency resolution pulls mdatp into the runtime image
issues.next_action_reason: Acknowledge the dependency-solve bug and discuss pinning explicit library providers / excluding mdatp in the runtime tdnf transaction.
issues.suggested_reply: Thanks @abjfh30 — good catch. `mdatp` advertising provides for `libcrypto.so.3`/`libssl.so.3`/`libcurl.so.4` letting the tdnf solver pick it as a provider for unrelated runtime deps is clearly unintended, and shipping Defender in the WSLg system distro is a real size/scope regression. We should pin the concrete Azure Linux library packages (or exclude `mdatp`) in the runtime stage's tdnf transaction. Would you be open to sending a PR against the Dockerfile runtime stage?
